en: You know, as Tom would say, you know, I think a lot of young actors, they, you know, they revere you, so they look to you.
ko: 톰이 말했듯이, 많은 젊은 배우들이 너를 존경해서 너를 바라본다는 걸 알아.

en: The other thing you inspire is impressions, people doing impressions.
ko: 톰이 말했듯이, 많은 젊은 배우들이 너를 존경해서 너를 바라본다는 걸 알아.

en: Now, when people do impressions of you, is it like sort of nails on a chalkboard, or do you kind of enjoy it?
ko: 또 한 가지는 사람들이 너의 인상을 따라 하는 거야.

en: No, I kind of enjoy it, especially if they do it well.
ko: 사람들이 너를 흉내낼 때, 그게 약간 칠판 긁는 소리 같아, 아니면 즐기는 편이야?

en: So, no, it's great. I love it.
ko: 아, 나는 즐기는 편이야, 특히 잘하면 더 좋아.

en: No, because any Hiddlestoners watching will know, Tom Hiddleston does impressions.
You do impressions of lots of people, so who is in your canon?
It's an expression of admiration, just to be clear.
ko: 톰 히들스턴은 여러 사람의 인상을 따라 해. 너는 누구를 흉내내?

en: So I do impressions of people that I admire, and I sort of listen to them.
ko: 톰 히들스턴은 여러 사람의 인상을 따라 해. 너는 누구를 흉내내?

en: The first time I did an impression that anyone was aware of,
I was just telling a story about Midnight in Paris, the Woody Allen film I was in,
and how I'd never read the whole script, and I thought it was set in the 20s.
ko: 내가 처음으로 인상을 따라 했던 건, 파리의 미드나잇에 대한 이야기였어.
